title: Agentic Human CAPTCHA
description: Keep in Survey: this is not embodied-core, but it has a concrete benchmark framing for distinguishing direct human input, HITL LLM use, and autonomous agents.
summary: Survey-stage extraction from the Notion “I’m not a Human CAPTCHA” page: build a benchmark that distinguishes direct human input, human-in-the-loop LLM use, and autonomous agent LLM behavior through process signals, pragmatic ambiguity, preference drift, planning footprint, and multi-agent safety probes.

details.text: Core benchmark idea: distinguish Human Input, Human-in-loop LLM, and Autonomous Agent LLM. The unresolved contribution is not another static safety set; it is process-aware behavioral classification under ambiguous, bilingual, and adversarial interaction.

Static and safety-probe references in the Notion page include Do Not Answer, SafetyPrompts, BeaverTails, JailbreakBench, StrongREJECT, IFEval, and PsySafe. These are useful as components, but they do not by themselves solve the human-vs-agent process classification problem.

Dynamic / social simulation angle: OASIS-style agent societies can generate interaction traces and adversarial variants, but the dashboard task should first define labels and signals before scaling simulation.

details: Open Notion to-dos folded here: optimize classifier method, integrate attack methods, control human ratio, add bilingual coverage, compare static vs dynamic evaluation pipelines, and decide which process signals can be collected ethically and reproducibly.

Notion migration addendum 2026-05-31: private proxy/config/setup notes were deliberately excluded from the public dashboard. Only the benchmark framing, labels, reference datasets, bilingual/static/dynamic evaluation ideas, and attack/defense split are retained.
risks_decisions: Keep secrets and local proxy/config notes out of the public dashboard. The card should contain benchmark design, not private setup commands.
Process signals can be privacy-sensitive; scope should prefer synthetic or consented traces and aggregate features.
The project is adjacent to safety/eval, not embodied robotics. Keep it survey unless it becomes a concrete benchmark paper or useful internal evaluator.
A strong baseline taxonomy is needed: text-only classifier, process-signal classifier, interaction-policy classifier, and adversarially prompted agent traces.

Survey disposition 2026-05-31: keep, because ArtPrompt/OpenCaptchaWorld/TuringBench-style references cover pieces, but they do not define the process-aware Human vs HITL-LLM vs autonomous-agent classification benchmark described here.
Next executable analysis: specify labels, observable process signals, bilingual probes, privacy constraints, and an attack/defense split before collecting any private data.
Archive if it loses the process-signal benchmark angle and becomes only another static LLM safety or CAPTCHA dataset.
Migration cleanup rule: keep process-signal benchmark design; never copy local proxy strings, credentials, or private deployment commands into dashboard state.
asset_alt: Process-aware Human CAPTCHA benchmark diagram comparing human, human-in-loop LLM, and autonomous agent traces
asset_caption: Survey diagram: behavioral process signals for classifying direct human input, HITL LLM use, and autonomous agents.
